findings.description: SearchableCountryDropdown, SearchableCountryWithCurrencyDropdown, SearchableCurrencySelect, and SearchableLanguageSelect share ~85% identical code (state, refs, three useEffects, handleKeyDown, select(), borderCls, dropdown shell). Extract a useSearchableDropdown hook (or generic SearchableDropdown<T> base) and have all four compose from it.

AUTOFILL_SYSTEM_PROMPT is a large prompt string embedded in gemini.ts (network layer). The resume-import prompt lives in prompt.ts with its own test file. Extract AUTOFILL_SYSTEM_PROMPT to src/resume-ai/autofillPrompt.ts to match the established pattern and enable independent testing.

AI high-confidence fills call saveLearnedMapping() directly (line 213) instead of saveElementMappings(), omitting nearbyText as a signal source and re-assembling signals manually. Replace with void saveElementMappings(domain, candidate.element, resp.profilePath) for consistency with the picker-selection path.
InfoTooltip is a reusable presentational component defined inside popup/App.tsx with no dependency on file scope. Move it to src/components/ui/InfoTooltip.tsx to enable reuse and avoid import cycles.

renderSection() is a large switch statement inlined in the component body. Extract it to a SectionRenderer component or getSectionComponent(id, props) lookup table to separate the routing concern from App layout.

SettingsSection is a 1140-line component with six distinct concerns: Appearance, AI Features, Export, Import, Cloud Backup, Reset. Consider splitting into sub-sections (AppearanceSection, AiFeaturesSection, DataManagementSection) composed in a parent Settings page.

PATH_FOCUS_TARGETS and FIELD_FOCUS_IDS are two parallel lookup tables serving the 'Go to Profile' deep-link CTA, mapping different keys to overlapping targets. As the schema grows this creates two update points. Consider a single source-of-truth record keyed by section+fieldId with derived indexes.

The fallback object in the AUTOFILL_FILL catch branch is a hardcoded zero-fill that duplicates the AutofillResult shape. If the type gains new fields the catch branch silently omits them. Import or reference an EMPTY_AUTOFILL_RESULT constant from src/autofill/index.ts.

autoprefixer is listed as a second PostCSS plugin after @tailwindcss/postcss. Tailwind CSS v4 already handles autoprefixing internally, so this is redundant. Remove autoprefixer from postcss.config.js and from package.json devDependencies.

The newEntryTick + requestAnimationFrame scroll-and-focus useEffect is duplicated verbatim across WorkHistorySection, EducationSection, LanguagesSection, SalarySection, WorkAuthorizationSection, and LinksSection — 6 identical copies. Extract a useScrollToNewEntry(ref, tick) hook.

The cls(err?) helper function (Tailwind class string for normal/error state) is copy-pasted identically in AddressSection, PersonalSection, LinksSection, WorkHistorySection, EducationSection, SalarySection, WorkAuthorizationSection, LanguagesSection, and DocumentsSection — 9 copies. Move to src/components/options/shared/fieldCls.ts and import it.

SearchableCountrySelect.filterCountries also searches by callingCode (for phone entry), while SearchableCountryDropdown only searches name and ISO. This meaningful behavioral difference is invisible from the names. Rename SearchableCountrySelect to SearchableCallingCodeSelect (or PhoneCountrySelect) to prevent future callers from choosing the wrong one.

handleSave calls saveProfile(merged) then immediately saveProfile(withDerived), two sequential writes per save. If the first write succeeds but derivation throws, the stored profile is correct but in-memory state (profile) is set to merged (without derived), creating a stale-state window. Consider computing derived fields before any storage write and only falling back if derivation fails.

detectAutoExpand() re-implements field signal extraction from a raw DOM element (querying name, id, placeholder, autocomplete, aria-label, label) instead of calling extractSignals() from signals.ts. Replace manual attribute reads with a call to extractSignals(element) to use the already-tested, maintained path.

fmtSalary() in picker.ts duplicates salary-formatting logic already in resolver.ts. Both produce "{amount} {currency}" via fmtAmount. Extract a shared formatSalaryAmount(amount, currency) helper (e.g. into utils/dateFormat.ts alongside fmtAmount) and use it in both files.

The fileChange FieldChange literal (lines 174-184) is duplicated nearly verbatim in handleRetry (lines 224-234). Extract a makeFileChange(file) helper to eliminate the duplication and prevent the two copies from diverging.

handleRetry duplicates the bulk of handleExtract's logic (abort controller setup, longWaitTimer, try/catch/finally, setChanges). The only difference is skipping the file-reading step. Refactor into a single runExtraction({ skipRead: boolean }) function that both paths call.

calculateCompletion() duplicates the validation logic already in getSectionCompletion(). The mandatory checks are written out twice in parallel. getSectionCompletion() should be called inside calculateCompletion() and the section booleans used to drive the check() calls.
check() and optCheck() are identically structured inner functions differing only in their target array. Extract a makeChecker(target) factory and call it twice to remove the structural duplication.

validateImportedProfile() rejects salary.current.amount of 0 (cur.amount > 0), but profileCompletion.ts accepts 0 as valid (amount >= 0). This inconsistency means a profile with amount=0 will fail re-import validation. The import validator should use cur.amount >= 0.

stripMarkdown is defined in gemini.ts (network layer) but is a pure text utility with no Gemini-specific logic. It should live in a shared utility module so it can be reused without importing from the network-layer file.

AIOptionPayload, AIFieldPayload, and AIFieldResponse are declared inside gemini.ts (network layer) but describe the autofill domain model. Move them to src/resume-ai/types.ts alongside FieldChange, ImportError, etc. to keep type declarations co-located.

professional.summary field def uses section: 'Work History' (line 172) and professional.noticePeriod uses the same (line 184). Both belong under a 'Professional' section heading in the diff UI, not 'Work History'. This mislabeling causes confusing grouping in the import review screen.

The 'Missing' dropdown trigger and panel are duplicated for the !isCoreComplete case and the isCoreComplete && optionalFieldsRemaining > 0 case. The only differences are panel heading text and dot color. Extract a MissingDropdown sub-component with those as props.

The optional prop is accepted but silently ignored — its JSDoc says 'the Optional tag is no longer rendered'. Remove the prop from the interface and update all callers that still pass optional. Dead prop creates confusion about whether it controls rendering.

Notice period validation logic (error message with pluralised unit) is inlined in the onChange handler for the value input, the onChange handler for the unit selector, and the validate() function — 3 separate copies. Extract a validateNoticeValue(value, unit) helper.

The load function inside the Drive state useEffect is wrapped in a named function then immediately called, and also assigned as handler. Just define handler once and call it directly — the intermediate load alias is noise.

handleImportReviewSave is a one-liner that just calls performImportSave. Since performImportSave already has the required signature, pass it directly to ImportReviewScreen's onSave prop and remove the wrapper.
handleGoToApiKey duplicates the body of the target === 'gemini-api-key' branch in applyFocusTarget (lines 112-115). Extract a shared focusGeminiKey() helper called by both.

handleNavigate is a one-liner wrapper around setActiveSection with a redundant cast. Accept SectionId directly in CompletionBanner and Sidebar prop types, or inline the cast at the call site, and remove handleNavigate.

sendToActiveTab is a regular async function inside the component recreated on every render. Wrap in useCallback with an empty dependency array so it isn't re-created and can safely be listed as a dependency in any future useEffect.

handleConfirmFill duplicates the fill-dispatch and result-handling logic from the 'nothing pre-filled' branch of handleAutofill (lines 156-163). Extract a shared dispatchFill(mode) helper that both branches call.

_learnedMappings is fetched with getLearnedMappings() inside executeAutofill() but never used (prefixed with _ and immediately discarded). This is a dead storage read on every autofill execution. Remove both the await call and the binding.

GEMINI_MODEL_PRIORITY includes 'gemini-3.1-flash-lite' and 'gemini-3.5-flash' but the comment says 'gemini-2.5-flash-lite' is a valid stored model NOT a probe candidate. If gemini-3.x models are also stored-only models, the comment is incomplete; if they are valid probe targets the comment is misleading. Document the relationship clearly.

getThemePreference() only checks 'light' and 'dark' explicitly, falling through to 'system' for everything else. 'system' stored explicitly is accepted via fallback implicitly. Add an explicit 'system' check to make the intent clear and prevent confusion if the type is extended.

font-weight: 650 and font-weight: 750 (lines 150, 237, 328) are not valid CSS font-weight values. Browsers round these to nearest valid values (600 and 800). Replace with standard values (600/700 or 700/800) to make intent explicit.

Two consecutive if(match.fieldPath) guards at lines 422 and 425 in the noData branch check the same condition independently. Merge into a single if block that pushes to both noDataFields and aiTextCandidates.
runAutofill() is exported as 'legacy single-phase entry point' but has zero callers outside this file. Remove it or add @deprecated JSDoc and document where it should be called from.

The resolve() private wrapper is a one-liner that only converts empty string from resolveProfileValue to null. Inline resolveProfileValue(profile, fieldPath) || null at each call site and remove the wrapper.

The setTimeout + document.addEventListener pattern for activeOutsideHandler registration is duplicated in both showNoDataCta() and showPicker(). Extract a registerOutsideHandler(handler) helper so the timing behaviour cannot diverge.
isVisibleInput() is a one-line wrapper that simply delegates to isElementVisible() with no additional logic. Remove it and call isElementVisible directly at its two call sites.

findBestOption() uses toLowerCase().trim() for matching instead of the project's canonical normalize() function from normalizer.ts. This is inconsistent and could miss options differing only in punctuation or hyphens. Replace with normalize() calls.

CONF_AI_YELLOW and CONF_CONTEXT both have value 0.70 and represent structurally different concepts. The comment correctly notes they 'can drift'. Add an explicit '// NOTE: intentionally equal today but conceptually separate' remark to make the duplication explicit.

The setValue helpers on every FieldDef entry repeat the same spread pattern ~20 times: { ...p, personal: { ...(p.personal ?? {} as Profile['personal']), field: v } }. A small patchNested(p, 'personal', { firstName: v }) helper would eliminate repetition and the forced casts.

GeminiModel includes 'gemini-3.5-flash' and 'gemini-3.1-flash-lite' which don't match any known Gemini naming convention (real models are gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-pro). If these are future placeholders comment them as such; if they are typos they are dead enum values that will fail at runtime.

COUNTRIES.sort() is called as a side effect at module load time, mutating the exported array after the popular-countries-pinned ordering is defined. The comment 'Popular countries pinned first' is misleading — the sort silently erases the pin order on load. Document whether alphabetical or pinned order is the intent.
VALID_VETERAN is reused for disabilityStatus validation. Rename to VALID_YES_NO_PNTS or introduce a separate VALID_DISABILITY constant with the same values so the intent is self-documenting.

Multipart boundary is generated with Date.now() + Math.random().toString(36). crypto.randomUUID() (already used in storage.ts for profile IDs) would be more robust and is consistent with how randomness is handled elsewhere.
merged.push([...current] as [number, number]) spreads an already-typed tuple unnecessarily. Push current directly to remove noise without any safety trade-off.

The GET_STATUS IIFE in the second useEffect has no cleanup guard. If the popup unmounts before the promise resolves, the state setter fires on an unmounted component. Add a cancelled flag or AbortController guard.

The jb:ai:nudge:dismissed session-storage read and getGeminiApiKey fetch are in two separate useEffect hooks that both run once on mount with no dependencies. Combining them into a single effect or Promise.all reduces async round-trips during popup initialization.

The 'No AI key' tooltip in the Autofill section header uses raw group/group-hover Tailwind classes instead of the InfoTooltip component defined in the same file. Refactor to use InfoTooltip for consistency.

sectionProps is reconstructed as a new object literal { profile, onSave: handleSave } on every render, causing every section component to receive a new prop reference even when profile and handleSave haven't changed. Wrap in useMemo.

The settings case in renderSection passes an inline arrow () => handleImportComplete(...) to onResetComplete, creating a new function reference every render. Extract it to a named handler (handleResetComplete) for stability.
_geminiModel (underscore prefix) is set and read in the debounce callback. The underscore suggests unused but it is actively read. Rename to geminiModel or activeGeminiModel for clarity.

DEFAULT_GEMINI_MODEL is hardcoded locally in SettingsSection. If this value is also defined in src/utils/storage.ts or src/resume-ai/gemini.ts, import from there rather than duplicating, to prevent drift when the model name changes.

The Work Arrangement radio group uses onChange={() => {}} as a no-op plus onClick for toggle logic. This is an accessibility anti-pattern: screen readers won't announce the radio as interactive. Use onChange for value change and track deselect separately.
checked={row.isCurrent ?? false} is redundant — isCurrent is guaranteed non-null by initRow (defaults to false) and emptyRow. Remove the ?? false.
isValidYear is defined inside the component body but doesn't close over any state. Hoist it to module scope to avoid re-creation on every render.

MONTHS in DateOfBirthPicker uses value: number (1-12) while MONTHS in MonthYearPicker uses value: string ('01'-'12'). Both contain the same 12 labels. Consider a shared MONTH_NAMES constant to prevent label drift.

normalizeExtractedProfile does two sequential spread-copy passes over the result (lines 108-114 for workHistory, lines 116-124 for professional.summary). These could be combined into a single object construction, reducing allocations.
fillDemoData() and clearDemoData() access form.elements without null-checking form first. If the element ID changes, both throw. Add if (!form) return; at the top of both functions.

The index % 7 === 0 heuristic to assign the 'review' CSS class has no comment explaining the intent (~14% yellow/review-state). Extract as a named constant or add a comment so future maintainers understand it is intentional.

globals.node is included for all TypeScript files. Extension source files run in the browser or service worker, not Node.js. Having Node globals active means ESLint won't flag accidental Node API usage in content scripts or popup code. Scope Node globals to config/build files only.

filterCountries(search) runs on every render and re-scans the full COUNTRIES array (200+ entries) synchronously. Wrap in useMemo(() => filterCountries(search), [search]). Same applies to SearchableCountryWithCurrencyDropdown, SearchableCurrencySelect, and SearchableLanguageSelect.

dictionaryExact() iterates all FIELD_DICTIONARY entries on every call. Since the dictionary is static, build a reverse-lookup Map<string, string> (term → fieldPath) once at module load. Reduces inner-loop cost from O(entries × variations) to O(1) per lookup.
calculateCompletion(profile) and getSectionCompletion(profile) are called unconditionally on every render, traversing the entire profile object. Wrap both in useMemo with profile as the dependency.

In check() inner function, groups.find((x) => x.sectionId === sectionId) performs a linear scan per failing check. With 15 checks this is negligible, but a Map<string, CompletionGroup> keyed by sectionId would be O(1). Same pattern in optCheck.

scanFields() calls getComputedStyle(el) and getBoundingClientRect() on every element during filter pass. Both force style recalculation/layout. Reorder checks: hidden attribute and disabled first (zero cost), then offsetParent, then getComputedStyle, then getBoundingClientRect only as last resort.

applyChanges calls FIELD_DEFS.find(d => d.id === change.id) inside a loop over changes — O(n²). Pre-build a Map<string, FieldDef> from FIELD_DEFS at module level (it's immutable) to make lookups O(1).
sectionsWithOptionalGaps and sectionFullCompletion are derived values rebuilt on every render. Combine into the same useMemo block as calculateCompletion/getSectionCompletion.

The color/colorMap derivation (lines 202-207) runs on every render even when percentage hasn't changed. Move into a useMemo or pure helper function to avoid re-creating the object on unrelated re-renders.
aiByFieldId is built with new Map(...) on every render. Wrap in useMemo with session.ai as the dependency.

runAIAutofill has no tests. Critical untested paths: high-confidence fills decrement lowConfidence/noData and increment noReview; low-confidence fills add a picker entry; aiGreenFilled set is populated for green fills; radio and checkbox fill paths; graceful return true when AI call throws.

No tests exist for any section component's validate() logic (e.g., WorkHistorySection's end-date-before-start-date check, EducationSection's year range boundary, PersonalSection's email regex). These are the highest-impact validation paths — a bug here silently stores corrupt data.

resolveFieldsWithAI and parseAutofillResponse have no test coverage. parseAutofillResponse is the critical boundary where AI output becomes trusted data. Missing: non-JSON returns [], invalid items filtered, items missing fieldId rejected, items with unexpected confidence rejected.

checkApiKey and validateApiKey have no test coverage. validateApiKey has non-trivial logic: iterates model priority list, distinguishes auth failures (401/403) from model-specific failures (404, 400 with non-key message). The 400-body-parse branch is especially hard to verify without tests.

The two-phase autofill flow (scan → confirming → fill with mode) is the primary user-facing interaction path with no unit or integration tests. Add tests for: (1) scan returning preFilledCount > 0 → confirming state, (2) merge dispatch, (3) overwrite dispatch, (4) error state on rejection.

retryPendingDriveSync has no tests. Its conditional logic (pendingSync flag, token presence, profile null-check) is testable with mocked storage and driveSync. Add: (1) no retry when pendingSync false, (2) no retry when token missing, (3) sync called when both present.

connectDrive() is not tested at all. Non-trivial branching: OAuth failure path, successful OAuth + Drive lookup failure path (writes pendingSync:true), full success path. Missing the 'connection succeeded but file lookup failed' branch.
saveLearnedMapping() has no test confirming behavior when chrome.storage.local.set fails (the rejected Promise propagation). The read-modify-write loop failure path is uncovered.

Layer 3 (fuzzy) test only asserts confidence > 0. Add two tests: one for strong fuzzy match (score > CONF_FUZZY_THRESHOLD) verifying confidence = score * CONF_FUZZY_STRONG_MULT, one for weak fuzzy match verifying CONF_FUZZY_WEAK_MULT.

fillFileField has no tests. It handles base64 decoding, MIME type parsing, DataTransfer reconstruction, and event dispatch — all with silent fallbacks. Add tests for: successful reconstruction dispatching input and change events; malformed data-URL prefix returns false; missing comma in base64 returns false.

clearHighlights() tests do not verify that data attributes (jbOrigBackground, jbOrigTransition) are removed from elements after clearing — only that background color is restored. Add assertions that confirm el.dataset.jbOrigBackground and el.dataset.jbOrigTransition are undefined after clearHighlights().

prompt.test.ts only checks surface-level string presence. It does not verify the emitted schema matches the Profile type (e.g., salary.current.country present, coverLetter absent per exclusion rule, education date rules stated). A schema-structure test would catch prompt/Profile drift earlier.

applyChanges with array-typed fields (workHistory, education, languages, workAuthorization, salary.expected) is not tested. Missing: verify accepting a workHistory conflict replaces the whole array, declining it leaves it intact.

Test environment is set to 'node' but the project tests DOM interactions (autofill, field scanning, filler, signals). jsdom and @types/jsdom are already installed. Switching to environment: 'jsdom' and adding a setupFiles entry for Chrome API stubs would make the test environment consistent.

The professional section of validateImportedProfile() (lines 120-128) is a pass-through with no structural validation of noticePeriod shape. Zero tests for importing a profile with professional.summary or professional.noticePeriod. Add tests for pass-through and verify non-object noticePeriod is excluded.
overwriteDriveWithLocal() is not tested at all. A simple test verifying it delegates to syncProfileToDrive and propagates the result would improve coverage.

revokeDriveToken() is not tested for the silent-failure path. The catch block at driveSync.ts:73 swallows network errors silently — add a test that confirms the error is swallowed and the function still resolves.
clearAllStorage() test verifies profile and learnedMappings are cleared but never tests that applicationHistory is also removed, even though it is in the storageRemove call.

resolvePathFocusTarget() is exported but has zero test coverage. The longest-prefix match loop (segments.pop() loop) is untested — a path like 'salary.expected.0.currency' should resolve to 'salary' via prefix walk.
formatISODate() is exported but completely untested. It performs local-calendar date formatting which differs from UTC-based toISOString(); the local-vs-UTC corner case is unverified.

The theme.test.ts beforeEach calls applyTheme('system') to reset state, which invokes matchMedia. If stubMatchMedia has not been called yet (first test in 'applyTheme(dark)' group), the call may hit the real jsdom matchMedia. Consider resetting via applyTheme('light') which never touches matchMedia.

scanRadioGroups() and scanCheckboxGroups() are not tested at all. Key behaviours to cover: groups with fewer than 2 options filtered for radio; consent checkboxes flagged by isConsent; anonymous checkboxes get synthetic keys; getGroupLegend returns fieldset legend.

workHistory.N.description and education.N.description fall through to the generic traversal path with no test. Add one test each to guard against a future accidental special-case breaking plain string sub-fields.
saveSection.test.ts doesn't test the optional duration parameter being forwarded to showToast. The third parameter could be silently dropped and tests would still pass.

No tests for the display function for any complex field (phone, noticePeriod, salary, workHistory, education). Display functions contain logic (replace, pluralisation, date formatting) that can silently break.
normalizeExtractedProfile tests don't cover workHistory and professional.summary present simultaneously. The current suite tests them in isolation only.
coverLetter pass-through in the documents section (profileValidator.ts line 320) is entirely untested — no test imports a profile with a documents.coverLetter field.

The lint script uses --ext .ts,.tsx which is an ESLint v8 flag. ESLint v9 flat config ignores --ext entirely — file targeting is done via the files glob in eslint.config.js. Remove --ext .ts,.tsx from both lint and lint:fix scripts.
autoprefixer is listed as a devDependency but Tailwind CSS v4 bundles its own vendor-prefix handling. Remove autoprefixer from package.json (already covered by f-0009 in architecture).

The @ alias is resolved to the project root (.) in vitest.config.ts. WXT already configures @ to src/ via its Vite/Vitest integration. Consider importing from wxt/testing instead of vitest/config to inherit WXT's alias and module stubs, avoiding drift between build-time and test-time resolution.
entrypoints/popup/App.css contains only a comment and is never imported anywhere (only style.css is imported in main.tsx). This file is dead — remove it.

SearchableCountrySelect imports findCountry from countries.ts; the other three Searchable components inline COUNTRIES.find() directly. Minor inconsistency — standardize on findCountry across all four for clarity.
